results.title: PH Deck Reviews (2026) | Product Hunt
results.content:
5.0

Based on 10 reviews

Review PH Deck?

Reviewers describe PH Deck as a simple, useful resource that makes things easier and surfaces helpful insights. Several comments are broad but positive, praising it as a strong problem-solving tool and a cool free resource. The most specific feedback says users learned from the insights it provides, but one reviewer also asks for source links behind numerical claims, noting concern about possible AI hallucinations. Overall, sentiment is clearly favorable, with the only recurring caveat being a desire for better citation and transparency.

results.title: Deck: The most capable AI assistant with its own inbox
results.content:
Subscribe

Launching today

Deck

# Deck

Launching today

## The most capable AI assistant with its own inbox

11 followers

## The most capable AI assistant with its own inbox

11 followers

Visit website

AI Chief Of Staff

Deck is an always-on AI assistant with its own email inbox. CC it on any thread or hand it a task, and it takes over. It learns your preferences and priorities over time, proactively flags what needs your attention, and can run on a schedule to deliver briefings and status updates automatically. It's built to take ambiguous, multi-step tasks head on, so you can move on with your day.

 Overview
 Reviews
 Alternatives
 Built with
 Team
 More

Launch tags:Productivity•Task Management•Artificial Intelligence

Launch Team / Built With

Clerk

Stripe

Loops

Subscribe [...] Hey all, I'm Stasia, founder of Deck. Deck is an AI teammate for busy operators, professionals, and teams. You can email it, use it in the app, sign it up for services you use, email alerts/newsletters, or set it up to run recurring work. It can read project files, summarize messy context, generate or edit decks/memos/sheets, answer questions over your knowledge base, and proactively surface things that need attention. Importantly, it doesn't require blanket inbox authorization to be effective and only emails you. We made email a first-class interface because delegation already happens there and it's a decades old, hardened protocol. People forward a thread, CC someone, attach files, subscribe it to email alerts and newsletters and ask for help. We believe Deck fits that behavior instead [...] ask for help. We believe Deck fits that behavior instead of asking users to move work into another chat app. But the system underneath is shared with the web app: automations, workflows, project search, document editing, and integrations. To get started, you name your assistant and claim its address and start free. We're letting PH users get in on the action with $15 off the monthly price using the promo code PRODUCTHUNT2026. Happy to answer anything about how it works, the trust model, or what we're learning from early users.
